summary: Matched the fighter animation routine ftAnim_8006EBE8, adding its implementation in ftanim.c and tightening nearby ftanim prototypes and struct/member types so the code can express JObj-based animation setup directly. The implementation resets/initializes root and animation skeleton JObjs, handles the no-blend versus blend paths separately, applies loop flags when fp->x594_b1 is set, sets HSD animation rates via HSD_ForeachAnim, and records blend frame state on the Fighter. The PR also updated scratches metadata, including marking func_8006EBE8 as OK. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.

key_files.role: Updated prototypes around ftAnim_8006EBE8 and related helper functions with more precise argument types.
Changed ftData nested x20->x8 from int to HSD_Joint*.
Adjusted a local item variable from int* to HSD_Joint** to align with corrected joint typing.
Simplified HSD_Type enum values after AOBJ_TYPE by relying on sequential enumeration.
Updated scratch status metadata, including func_8006EBE8 becoming OK.

what_changed: Added ftAnim_8006EBE8(HSD_GObj* gobj, float arg1, float anim_rate, float anim_blend_frames) to src/melee/ft/ftanim.c.
The new routine gets root_jobj with GET_JOBJ(gobj), gets Fighter with GET_FIGHTER(gobj), and uses fp->x8AC_animSkeleton as the animation skeleton JObj.
Both root_jobj and anim_jobj are passed through ftAnim_80070758 before the blend/no-blend branch.

For anim_blend_frames == 0, the code calls ftAnim_8006FA58(fp, true, fp->x108_costume_joint->child), ftAnim_8006FE08(fp, false), applies ftAnim_80070710 to root_jobj, optionally sets AOBJ_LOOP on root_jobj animations, and applies anim_rate to root_jobj animations.

For nonzero anim_blend_frames, the code calls ftAnim_8006FB88(fp, true, fp->x108_costume_joint->child), ftAnim_8006FE08(fp, true), applies ftAnim_80070710 to both anim_jobj and root_jobj, optionally sets AOBJ_LOOP on both animation trees, and applies anim_rate to both.
The implementation stores anim_blend_frames in fp->x8A4_animBlendFrames and clears fp->x8A8_unk to 0.
ftanim.h changed ftAnim_8006EBE8's first parameter from Fighter_GObj* to HSD_GObj* and named the anim_rate and anim_blend_frames parameters.
ftanim.h added a declaration for ftAnim_8006FE08(Fighter* fp, bool).

ftanim.h corrected helper prototypes so ftAnim_8006FA58 and ftAnim_8006FB88 use bool and HSD_Joint* rather than looser int placeholders, and ftAnim_80070010/ftAnim_80070108 use HSD_Joint* for their joint argument.
types.h changed a nested ftData field x20->x8 from int to HSD_Joint*.
ftSk_SpecialS.c changed a local variable from int* item to HSD_Joint** item, consistent with the corrected joint-pointer interpretation.
object.h changed HSD_Type enum entries after AOBJ_TYPE = 1 from explicit numeric assignments to implicit sequential values.
scratches.txt changed a large contiguous block of scratch records from percentage statuses to OK, with func_8006EBE8 specifically changing from 100% to OK.
smart_moves: Used existing baselib animation traversal APIs instead of hand-walking all object animation nodes: HSD_ForeachAnim is called with JOBJ_TYPE, a combined mask, HSD_AObjSetFlags, and HSD_AObjSetRate.
Encoded the repeated animation object mask as a static const u32, keeping the large bitmask readable and avoiding recomputation/noise in the matched function body.

Separated the no-blend and blend paths cleanly, matching the behavioral difference that the blend path updates both fp->x8AC_animSkeleton and the root JObj while the no-blend path only updates root_jobj for timing/rate.
Improved types in headers and related caller code so the matched function can use HSD_Joint* and bool directly instead of placeholder int arguments.
Kept the public prototype compatible with generic HSD_GObj usage, matching how callers such as ftSk_SpecialS already receive an HSD_GObj*.
decomp_lessons: When a match depends on animation tree APIs, first make the involved skeleton/costume fields real HSD_Joint* or HSD_JObj* types; placeholder ints can force awkward casts or mismatched codegen.

For HSD_ForeachAnim calls, the object type and mask constants matter: this match used JOBJ_TYPE plus a mask covering AOBJ, COBJ, DOBJ, FOBJ, FOG, JOBJ, LOBJ, POBJ, ROBJ, WOBJ, RENDER, CHAN, TEVREG, and CBOBJ.
Boolean helper parameters in animation setup functions should be typed as bool when the code is really passing true/false; this can clarify branch intent and improve matching ergonomics.
Blend-frame state in Fighter is updated after the setup branch, not inside either branch: fp->x8A4_animBlendFrames receives the requested blend frames and fp->x8A8_unk is cleared.
Enum cleanup is acceptable when preserving numeric values through sequential order; object.h kept AOBJ_TYPE = 1 and allowed following HSD_Type members to auto-increment.
naming_conventions: Function name retained the address-based convention: ftAnim_8006EBE8.
New local names describe inferred roles rather than offsets only: root_jobj, anim_jobj, anim_rate, and anim_blend_frames.
Fighter fields still use project offset-style names such as x8AC_animSkeleton, x108_costume_joint, x8A4_animBlendFrames, x8A8_unk, and x594_b1.
Header comments keep address prefixes such as /* 06EBE8 */ for ftanim declarations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Represented the long HSD_ForeachAnim mask as a static const u32 mask, likely matching the compiler's handling of a reused constant expression.
Used exact branch structure around anim_blend_frames == 0 to distinguish immediate animation setup from blended animation setup.
Called HSD_ForeachAnim separately for anim_jobj and root_jobj in the blend path, rather than trying to abstract the duplicated calls, preserving likely source/codegen shape.
Used AOBJ_ARG_AU with HSD_AObjSetFlags/AOBJ_LOOP and AOBJ_ARG_AF with HSD_AObjSetRate, matching the expected callback argument modes.
Adjusted prototypes and field types before or alongside the function match so calls could be written naturally without extra casts.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftAnim_8006EBE8 or identify the semantic name of arg1.
Search for later cleanup of fp->x8A8_unk and fp->x594_b1 to understand their animation-state roles.
Look for PRs matching ftAnim_8006FA58, ftAnim_8006FB88, ftAnim_8006FE08, or ftAnim_80070710 for additional context on this animation setup sequence.
Check whether the broad scratches.txt OK normalization around 0x800679B0-0x8006EBE8 was intentional metadata cleanup or generated by tooling.
